text:
Just a genuine question, with no disrespect or criticism toward anyone or any skin color.
I’m simply surprised by the concept. The movie is called Snow White, and the name literally refers to someone with very fair skin.
So I’m wondering why the character’s appearance is being changed like this, similar to what happened with Cinderella and Ariel.
Ariel’s story, for example, is originally linked to Denmark, which is part of its cultural background.
I’m absolutely against racism and I support diversity, but does that require completely changing established folklore and heritage?
And just to be clear: I’m not blonde, I’m from the Middle East, I’m only asking this out of genuine curiosity.
author: @Dodo_hanna

text:
Finally got around to watching it, and honestly—this was not Snow White.

The casting choice for young and adult Snow White felt off. Rachel is of mixed heritage, with a Colombian mother and a Polish father. Emilia is of mixed ethnicity—her father is American and her mother is Mexican—making her Latina/Hispanic and Caucasian, which doesn’t align with the character as traditionally portrayed. The Evil Queen was also far too camp for my liking. Jonathan and his bandits had a hint of Robin Hood and his Merrie Men about them, but instead of robbing the rich to help the poor, they did whatever was necessary to survive.

Not casting actors who are actually dwarfs as the Seven Dwarfs was strange, and rebranding them as “magical beings” felt like an unnecessary and awkward change.

I also think the film should have stayed much closer to the original. Snow White meeting the Prince in the forest, with him overhearing her sing “Someday My Prince Will Come,” is an iconic moment that didn't needed to be taken out. In the original, the Huntsman places a pig’s heart in the box—changing this to an apple in the remake was yet another alteration that added nothing. The transformation from Evil Queen to old hag should have been intensified, making her appearance far more grotesque. Additionally, when the Evil Queen disguised herself as a peddler woman, the sky should have darkened to a cloudy gray, with thunder and lightning as she approached the cottage, heightening the visual impact and ominous atmosphere. Leaving out the glass coffin was another element that weakened the film.

text:
I honestly like live action version.
Even though many people dislike it, I like it because this story has a very realistic and practical ending.

Think about it for a moment:
A princess — the king’s only daughter, the only heir of the kingdom — finds her true love. But after finding true love, she goes with that prince to his kingdom, abandoning her motherland just to live a beautiful life.
Is that right?

Snow White was born into the responsibility of her own kingdom. As a princess, she was meant to carry that weight and protect her people as their rightful queen. What she should not do is run away from those responsibilities. But in the original story, she leaves with a prince who is about 14 years older than her, and we don’t even know what happened to her kingdom afterward.

Her people suffered for a long time under an evil queen. Then Snow White also leaves — the throne becomes empty. That could bring great danger to the helpless people, and the kingdom might even fall into a civil war I think 19s people made that story to enhance the idea that Women are not for rule and they mean to be saved by a man in the end or whatever. But in the live-action story, Snow White doesn’t run away even though she had plenty of chances to.
She found her true love who saved her life, but she never abandoned her motherland or her duty. In the end, she becomes the queen her people needed. That is the correct and most beautiful ending the Snow White story deserves.

Also, this story teaches us that beauty is not what appears on the outside — it is something that comes from within. Many people say the Queen in this version is more beautiful than Snow White, and because of that, they hate the movie. But the story teaches that real beauty is not our outer appearance, but the character and goodness that come from inside.

text: Галь Гадот очень грациозная и красивая, то как она двигается завораживает. Но ее типаж это точно не злодейка, она милая, в ней нет ничего пугающего. Джонатан был милый, сильно спасал своей харизмой сцены, где присутствовал. Белоснежка, ну тут я скажу одно, я очень сильно обрадовалась когда она съела отравленное яблоко , и на этом фильм можно было бы закончить, я бы поставила ему 10. На роль белоснежки наняли худшую из все возможных актрис. Она реально страшная, ее челюсть живёт отдельной от нее жизнью. Сам персонаж получился откровенно тупым, она съела яблоко от неизвестной старушки которой явно не доверяла, потому что та сказала "открой ротик и скушай за папу", если проследить за ее действиями, то она точно страдает умственной отсталостью! Еще она неблагодарная , ее гномы приютили, а она даже не помогала им в уборке или готовке, только команды раздавала. А люди в городе пошли за ней с хера ли? Она даже слова им не сказала, никакой речи не произнесла. А насчёт гномов, фиг с их внешностью, зачем их сделали чуть ли не врагами, и зачем там был буллинг просточка? В оригинале они лучше друзья, и даже в мыслях у них нету драк и буллинга. Но я считаю, что все можно было бы простить, и исправить этот провал просто заменив главную актрису, именно она вызывает такое дикое отторжение, что я уверена, все кто смотрел этот фильм, болел за злую королеву. Если бы там не было Рейчел Зеглер, то фильм бы прошёл бесследно как и "Русалочка", принеся минимальные потери.
